thread.title: Surgeon General advocates for fluoride-free Florida
thread.title_full: Surgeon General advocates for fluoride-free Florida

author: Grace Bellinghausen
title: Surgeon General advocates for fluoride-free Florida
text:
TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (CBS12) — Florida's Surgeon General Joseph Ladapo announced Friday he would be recommending against water fluoridation citing "neuropsychiatric risk."
According to the Florida Department of Health (FDOH), fluoride is a naturally occurring chemical compound found in groundwater, fresh and salt water, rainwater, soil plants, and foods.
Water fluoridation has long been seen as a game-changer, democratizing access to tooth decay-fighting chemicals. Yet, as fluoride-packed dental products become increasingly widespread, and as U.S. cities and numerous European nations call it quits, Ladapo says he denounces the practice and sees the concerns.
See also: Vero Beach airport gains three new routes
"Adding fluoride to water increases the risk of neuropsychiatric disease in children and reduces their IQ," Ladapo wrote in a post to X . "We can strengthen teeth without consuming this neurotoxin."
According to the FDOH, Floridians should be aware of safety concerns related to systemic fluoride exposure including:
Adverse effects in children reducing IQ, cognitive impairment, and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder. Increased neurobehavioral problems in children whose mothers ingested fluoride during pregnancy. Accumulation of fluoride in the pineal gland causes sleep cycle disturbances. Skeletal fluorosis, which could increase the risk of bone fracture. Find more ways to stay up to date with your latest local news.
